metadata.summary: Agentic ideation pipeline: generation → semantic dedup → cluster-and-score; emits a scored shortlist for human review, not a facilitated session.

content.title: Brainstorming Ideation (Agentic Pipeline)
content.summary: Brainstorming-ideation covers using brainstorming techniques as a generative input for automated pipelines — where the output is a scored shortlist, not a facilitated group session. The agent receives a problem statement, runs a structured generation pass (Classic, Reverse, or Brainwriting-simulated), deduplicates semantically, clusters by theme, and scores against an impact/effort rubric. For facilitated group sessions, see brainstorming-techniques/ which covers the human-facilitation angle.

why.p: Agent-generated idea lists plateau in novelty around 30-40 ideas without persona switching or domain constraint injection. The critical failure points are: (1) mixing generation and evaluation in the same prompt — causes premature convergence; (2) using the generating agent to also score ideas — circular bias toward first-pass outputs; (3) semantic clustering silently merging two distinct viable ideas — human review at the cluster stage is mandatory before scoring.

list.item: Product feature generation: given a persona + pain point, produce candidate features to evaluate against backlog
Content ideation: bulk-generating article topics, campaign angles, or ad copy variants for human selection
Risk surfacing: Reverse Brainstorming to enumerate failure modes before a launch or deployment
Feeding a scored shortlist into a downstream pipeline (Linear backlog, Notion idea database)
Feeding an ideation pipeline where the output is a scored shortlist, not a facilitated group session
When human group dynamic is the point (team alignment, buy-in, psychological safety) — agentic bulk generation skips the relational work
When the problem is too narrow for divergent thinking; use direct prompting or SCAMPER instead
When ideas require domain-expert validation that an LLM cannot reliably provide (medical, legal, engineering safety)
When you need the dot-voting or scoring to be done by the same agent that generated the ideas — circular bias invalidates the prioritization

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: Three-pass pipeline: diverge (generation only, no evaluation), semantic dedup, cluster-and-score. Each pass is a separate prompt or function call — never combined in one.

Agent receives a problem statement, a target idea count, and an optional technique flag (classic / reverse / brainwriting-simulated). It runs a generation pass with enforced formatting (one idea per line, no justification), then a dedup pass (semantic similarity), then a cluster-and-score pass. Output is a scored shortlist (top 5-10) with cluster labels, ready for human review. The human selects finalists and the agent generates one-line implementation notes per finalist.

Recommended subagents: faion-brainstorm — purpose-built for structured diverge/converge; use when full multi-round review is needed. General Claude Opus call — for raw generative passes where creativity > structure.

rule.statement: Send the generation prompt with only the problem statement and constraints. Do NOT include scoring criteria, evaluation language, or ranking instructions. The output must be a numbered list with no justification text.
rule.rationale: Including evaluation language in the generation prompt causes premature convergence — the model self-censors and produces fewer, safer ideas than an unconstrained generation pass.

rule.statement: Seed the generation pass with persona injection to force diverse viewpoints: "Generate from the perspective of a skeptic, an optimist, a first-time user, and an engineer." This produces more diverse output than an anonymous generation pass.
rule.rationale: Agent-generated ideas plateau in novelty around 30-40 without persona switching. Training data bias causes clustering around the most common solutions in the domain.

rule.statement: Remove near-duplicate ideas before clustering using semantic similarity (cosine similarity threshold 0.82 with sentence-transformers). Do not rely on exact string matching — paraphrased duplicates survive it.
rule.rationale: Duplicate ideas inflate cluster size and give false confidence in idea frequency. A cluster with 8 ideas that are all paraphrases of one idea is not 8 independent signals.

rule.statement: Cluster deduplicated ideas into 5-7 semantic themes in a separate pass. Score each cluster on Impact (3x weight) and Feasibility (2x weight). Return a scored shortlist of top 5-10 clusters for human review.
rule.rationale: Scoring before clustering produces per-idea scores that ignore the compound value of a theme. Scoring after clustering evaluates the best implementation of each direction.

rule.statement: Require human review of cluster boundaries before finalizing scores. The clustering pass can silently merge two distinct viable approaches that share domain vocabulary.
rule.rationale: Semantic clustering by cosine similarity groups by surface word overlap, not conceptual distinctiveness. Two approaches can share words while being strategically opposed.

rule.statement: For risk surfacing: invert the problem ("How would we make X fail?"), generate negative ideas, then reverse each into a positive mitigation. Run as two sequential passes — inversion then reversal — not combined.
rule.rationale: Groups generate more honest and specific negative ideas than positive ones, especially when they are politically constrained from criticizing directly.

section.name: prompt-patterns
section.prompt: Generate <N> distinct ideas for: <PROBLEM_STATEMENT>. Constraints: <CONSTRAINTS if any>. Format: numbered list only. No explanations. No duplicates.
List <N> ways to make <PROBLEM_AREA> as bad as possible. Be specific. Format: numbered list only. Then reverse each item into a solution in a second numbered list.

antipattern.description: Using the same agent that generated the ideas to also dot-vote or rank them.
antipattern.reason: The generating agent has higher confidence in ideas it produced with higher certainty in the first pass. Self-scoring compounds this into a self-reinforcing ranking with no independent judgment.

rule.statement: Scoring must be done by a separate agent or human. The generating agent provides the list; a fresh agent or human applies the scoring rubric.
rule.rationale: Separation of generation and evaluation is the most important architectural rule in agentic brainstorming pipelines.

antipattern.description: Including evaluation language ("good idea because...", "this works better than option B") in the generation prompt.
antipattern.reason: The model self-censors and produces fewer, safer ideas. The generation pass becomes a mixed pass that produces a filtered list masquerading as a brainstorm.

rule.statement: Generation prompt must contain only: problem statement, constraints, format instructions, and quantity target. No evaluation language.
rule.rationale: Strict format enforcement (numbered list only, no explanations) prevents evaluation language from contaminating output even when the model defaults to justification.

antipattern.description: Accepting cluster assignments without human review, particularly when two conceptually distinct approaches share domain vocabulary.
antipattern.reason: Cosine similarity clusters by word overlap. "Automate onboarding email sequence" and "Automate onboarding task assignment" may cluster together despite being architecturally different solutions.

antipattern.description: Passing a list of 50+ ideas to a scoring prompt in a single call.
antipattern.reason: Ideas in positions 1-15 receive systematically higher scores than identical ideas in positions 35-50 due to positional bias in LLM attention. The first batch is not comparable to the last batch.

rule.statement: Chunk idea lists into batches of 25 for scoring. Score each batch against the rubric independently, then compare top-scored ideas across batches in a final pass.
rule.rationale: Chunking equalizes positional treatment. Cross-batch comparison in the final pass is a manageable 25-item set with no positional artifact.

list.item: jq — Parse and deduplicate JSON idea lists from agent output. Built-in most Unix systems.
sentence-transformers (Python) — Semantic deduplication of idea lists. Install: pip install sentence-transformers
Miro (SaaS, partial REST API) — Sticky-note creation via API; cluster grouping is manual
Notion (SaaS, REST API) — Ideal for logging scored idea shortlists
Linear (SaaS, REST API) — Convert top ideas directly into backlog issues
Airtable (SaaS, REST API) — Tabular idea scoring with weighted formula columns

section.name: semantic-dedup-pipeline

list.item: Always separate the generation prompt from the evaluation prompt — same-call mixing causes premature pruning.
Inject domain constraints into the generation prompt rather than the evaluation prompt: "ideas must be implementable by a 2-person team" up front produces better raw lists than filtering later.
Run a Reverse Brainstorm variant on the same problem as a sanity check — if the negatives are hard to reverse into positives, the problem framing may be wrong.
Store raw idea lists (pre-dedup, pre-score) in a scratch doc for future re-use; today's rejected idea may be valuable in a different context.
"Brainwriting simulation" (agent pretends to be 6 different people passing ideas) consistently produces less diverse output than a single well-seeded prompt with persona injection.
Dot voting by the same agent that generated the ideas is circular — the agent will favor ideas it generated with higher confidence.
Human checkpoint required before finalizing shortlist: the agent's semantic clustering can silently merge two distinct viable ideas into one cluster, eliminating a viable option.
Long idea lists (50+) that get passed to a scoring prompt often see the first 10-15 ideas scored more generously due to positional bias.
Agent-generated idea lists are biased toward patterns already common in training data; genuinely novel ideas require cross-domain prompting or persona injection.
Brainwriting round-by-round simulation loses the "build on others" benefit because the agent reads its own prior ideas, creating an echo chamber.
Convergence (clustering, dot voting, scoring) is where agent errors compound — misclassified clusters lead to wrong shortlists.
Quantity is trivially achieved (100 ideas in seconds) but quality filtering requires human judgment at the evaluation gate.

template.purpose: Reverse Brainstorm prompt with inversion step
Sentence-transformers semantic deduplication for idea lists
